semanticCore: Restraining, marshalling, grouping, keeping back, or inspiration
quranicRelevance: Soldiers marshalled/kept in ranks (Yooza'oon - 'fahum yooza'oon'), and inspiring hearts to gratitude (Awzi'nee - 'rabbi awzi'nee').

keyDerivations.meaning: To restrain, check, marshal, hold back, or keep in ranks
To inspire, prompt, grant self-control, make adhere, or direct
They are kept in check, marshalled in ranks, or held back

figurativeOrRareUsages: رَبِّ أَوْزِعْنِي أَنْ أَشْكُرَ نِعْمَتَكَ (27:19): My Lord, inspire/prompt me (grant me the self-control/restraint) to be grateful for Your favor (Solomon's prayer).
discardedNoise: Distribution of inheritance shares (Tawzee') in estate law.
Obscure details of camel ranks in long caravans.
summaryEn: The root represents marshalling and inspired restraint, detailing Solomon's army kept in check (Yooza'oon) and his prayer for self-restraint (Awzi'nee) to remain grateful.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الكف والمنع والترتيب، واصفاً ترتيب وحشد جيش سليمان لئلا يتفرق (يوزعون)، ودعائه لربط قلبه بضبط النفس وشكر النعمة (أوزعني).
rawText: الوَزَعَةُ i. q. أَعْوَانُ الرَّجُلِ. (TA in art. امل.) وازِعٌ: see فَارِعٌ.
